title: Lavonte David Confident in Bucs’ Wide Receivers
description: Despite the departure of Mike Evans, the retired linebacker believes a healthy Chris Godwin and a deep rotation of emerging young talents will keep Tampa’s offense explosive.
fullText:
Former Tampa Bay Buccaneers linebacker Lavonte David has certainly been making the rounds with the media lately.

Even though he retired this offseason after a legendary 14-year career with Tampa, the franchise icon still has very strong feelings about the Bucs' upcoming season.

David has already said the Bucs will be "animals" this year, and recently made headlines talking about his desire to play with star rookie Rueben Bain Jr., leading many to question whether he could change his mind about retirement.

Now David is expressing his feelings about the Bucs' wide receivers, who have enormous shoes to fill after Mike Evans left Tampa for the San Francisco 49ers in free agency.

Even without Evans, the Bucs' WR room appears to be in good hands, with emerging star Emeka Egbuka looking to build upon a promising rookie season and reliable veteran Chris Godwin Jr. back healthy after missing 17 games over the past two seasons with injury.

Third-year WR Jalen McMillan is also healthy again after missing most of 2025 with a scary neck injury. McMillan is looking to pick up where he left off as a rookie, when he became one of quarterback Baker Mayfield's favorite weapons.

Add in rookie Ted Hurst, whom the Bucs drafted in the third round, and second-year WR Tez Johnson, and Tampa has a receiver room that looks both talented and deep.

David agrees. As reported by Mason Riney of Buccaneers Wire, the former leader of Tampa's defense has expressed his confidence in the Bucs' receivers for 2026.

Though losing Evans will surely hurt the offense, David believes Tampa's other receivers can step up in his absence.

Appearing on The Jim Rome Show, David thinks, collectively, the group can make up for the loss of Evans.

"You've got a receiving room who can fill the void of losing Mike Evans. You won't say everyone can replace Mike, but it's a collective group that can do a lot of great things."

Of course, none of the Bucs' receivers will be expected to replace Evans on his own, but having five different guys who have the talent that Tampa's receivers all possess makes the position one of the strongest on the team.

Tampa's young wide receivers have already gained valuable experience being forced into large roles early in their careers.

Egbuka became the Bucs' de facto WR1 last season after injuries to Evans, McMillan, and Godwin. Johnson also became a major piece of the offense after the injuries, taking full advantage of his increased opportunities.

McMillan had to step in for Godwin as a rookie in 2024 and instantly formed a strong duo with Evans and built a connection with Mayfield.

Mayfield already has trust in and a rapport with all four of Tampa's top receivers. Hurst is looking to add himself to the mix as a rookie, and he brings a great combination of size and speed to the table.

It is very easy to see why David has so much confidence in the Bucs' receivers for the upcoming season.

title: 'Rangers lead chase for Sturm's Fosso' - gossip
description: Rangers are reportedly leading the chase to sign Ryan Fosso from Sturm Graz, while Dundee United's Will Ferry is linked with Celtic and Aberdeen are said to want Hibernian's Chris Cadden.
fullText:
Rangers are reportedly leading the chase to sign Ryan Fosso from Sturm Graz, while Dundee United's Will Ferry is linked with Celtic and Aberdeen are said to want Hibernian's Chris Cadden.

Rangers are leading the chase for Sturm Graz defensive midfielder Ryan Fosso, but Levante have also tabled an offer to the 23-year-old's representatives while Torino are among Italian clubs interested. (Sky Sports)

Jens Hjerto-Dahl wishes to have more time to consider his options after Cardiff City tabled an improved bid worth £7.8m - £6.2m initially with £1.6m in add-ons - for the 20-year-old Tromso midfielder who previously expressed a preference to join Rangers. (iTromso)

Cardiff City's increased offer for Jens Hjerto-Dahl is likely to be big enough to be accepted by Tromso should minor tweaks be made, with Rangers' interest appearing to cool as they progress alternative midfield targets. (Nordlys)

Rangers are set to formalise their interest in Brighton & Hove Albion winger Amario Cozier-Duberry after Anderlecht had a £5m bid rejected and with Hull City, Paris FC, Red Bull Salzburg and West Ham United also keen on the 21-year-old. (Simon Collings on X)

Rangers manager Derek McInnes reckons two signings could be completed before Sunday's friendly against West Ham United, with another potentially following before next Friday's league opener against Dundee United. (Scottish Sun)

James Penrice's representatives say Rangers are not interested in the AEK Athens right-back but there is a small window of opportunity for Celtic to sign the Scot. (Box2Box)

Celtic are in talks with AEK Athens' former Heart of Midlothian left-back, James Penrice, with the 27-year-old open to a return to Scotland even though he would be back-up to Kieran Tierney. (TeamTalk)

Dundee United 25-year-old Will Ferry is on a list of potential left-back options Celtic manager Martin O'Neill is considering. (The Courier)

Thursday's English & European transfer rumours

Who has your Premiership club brought in & let go this summer?

Japan forward Daizen Maeda is scheduled to have a medical with Ipswich Town ahead of the 28-year-old completing his transfer from Celtic for an initial £8m plus £2m in add-ons. (Sky Sports)

Paderborn head coach Ralf Kettemann says the ball is in the Bundesliga club's court as they negotiate with Celtic over the Scottish champions' desire to sign 22-year-old midfielder Mika Baur. (NW Sport via Scottish Sun)

Kelechi Iheanacho, the 29-year-old striker who had been offered a new contract by Celtic, says he instructed his agent to do everything possible to thrash out a move to Bursaspor as he was excited by the prospect of leading them back to the Turkish Super Lig. (Daily Record)

Calvin Stengs, the 27-year-old midfielder who had been linked with a move to Celtic last summer but instead joined Pisa on loan, has become a free agent after agreeing to a mutual termination of his contract with Feyenoord. (Glasgow Times)

Dundee United have held talks with Baseksehir about taking 19-year-old midfielder Abdoulaye Yoro on loan. (Daily Record)

Aberdeen have expressed an interest in 29-year-old Hibernian right-back Chris Cadden. (Sky Sports)

Former Scotland striker and ex-Rangers manager Ally McCoist has admitted he would never turn down the chance to be national head coach as the hunt for Steve Clarke's successor continues. (TalkSport)

title: Dricus du Plessis’ Coach: He Could Have Finished Kamaru Usman
description: Morne Visser, the coach of former UFC middleweight champion Dricus du Plessis, believes he could have finished Kamaru Usman at UFC OKC.Du Plessis won a dominant five-round decision over Usman in what was the Fight of the Night at UFC Oklahoma City, with scorecards of 50-45, 49-46, and 49-46 in the South African’s favor.But despite […] The post Dricus du Plessis’ Coach: He Could Have Finished Kamaru Usman appeared first on HEAVY.
fullText:
Morne Visser, the coach of former UFC middleweight champion Dricus du Plessis, believes he could have finished Kamaru Usman at UFC OKC.

Du Plessis won a dominant five-round decision over Usman in what was the Fight of the Night at UFC Oklahoma City, with scorecards of 50-45, 49-46, and 49-46 in the South African’s favor.

But despite the dominating victory, du Plessis was not able to finish Usman during the fight despite hurting the former UFC welterweight champion multiple times.

As far as du Plessis’ coach is concerned, however, a finish was there for the taking, but du Plessis showed a ton of respect towards Usman and never really pressed for the finish when he hurt him.

Speaking to Submission Radio following UFC OKC, Visser explained why du Plessis did not finish Usman despite having him on the ropes several times during the bout.

“The game plan was 100 percent to completely stay composed, and that’s what (du Plessis) did. Obviously he listens very well to me, so I’ve never ever pushed him through the whole fight to push for the finish. I saw that Kamaru does recover very quickly after getting hit that hard and punched that hard in the face. I mean, he was out a couple of times on his feet, but I shouted very loudly to Dricus just to stay composed and pick the shots because a guy who recovers that quickly is dangerous. He’s desperate,” Visser said (via MMAjunkie.com).

“So, I am super, super happy with our results. I think this is the best I’ve seen Dricus ever or the world has seen him ever. This was a high-pressure fight. I mean, this was our comeback fight after 10, 11 months off. So, I’m very happy. And he listened. We never pushed for anything. We never pushed once for the finish, not even once. I mean, you saw Kamaru complaining about a low blow and the thumb or the finger, all nonsense. Dricus could have finished him there, but he was just not pushing. He was just respectful.”

Following his win over Usman at UFC OKC, du Plessis has reaffirmed his spot as one of the top middleweights in the sport, but he may have to wait a while before he gets a title shot against current UFC middleweight champion Sean Strickland, just because of how backlogged the division is right now.

Strickland is likely going to fight Nassourdine Imavov in his next bout, though former champ Khamzat Chimaev also wants a rematch with Strickland. Not to mention Joe Pyfer is another top contender who is due for a big fight, plus you have guys like Brendan Allen and Caio Borralho also right behind them.

Du Plessis seems unlikely to get the next title shot against Strickland, then, and will likely have to fight someone like Allen or Pyfer in his next bout before he potentially fights Strickland in a middleweight title trilogy bout.

title: Asia Tour 2026: Remembering Sun Jihai’s City career
description: Ahead of our Asia Tour this summer, we reflect on an Asian player who made a big impact in east Manchester… Men's Team How to follow City on this summer’s Asia Tour We’ll return to South Korea t...
fullText:
Ahead of our Asia Tour this summer, we reflect on an Asian player who made a big impact in east Manchester…

We’ll return to South Korea this time around to face a K League All Stars side and Atletico Madrid after facing Inter in Hong Kong.

And our 2023 trip to Japan and South Korea proved our incredible popularity on that side of the world.

However, early in the noughties, much of our supporter base in Asia came from Chinese fans.

Sun Jihai was a versatile operator, capable of playing anywhere across defence or midfield.

While a determined but unglamorous figure on the pitch, he was a superstar back home.

China, the second most populous country on Earth, has a deep-rooted passion for Premier League football. Despite that, it hasn’t ever proved successful in producing many players for the elite European leagues.

So when Sun became City’s first ever Asian player with a £2 million move from Dalian Shide in 2002, the excitement in China was well documented.

Alongside Fan Zhiyi, he had been the first Chinese man to play in England when on loan at Crystal Palace in 1998.

Now he was in England permanently with a side that was destined for promotion to the top flight.

A solid first few months followed as Kevin Keegan’s Blues swept away all the competition to finish top of Division One and seal a return to the promised land.

He would return to East Asia that summer as one of the stars of China’s squad at the 2002 World Cup in Japan and South Korea. Unfortunately, an early injury in their opener ruled him out of the group stage as China crashed out.

However, back in Manchester for the start of the next season, Sun was immediately a first choice at right-back for Keegan.

In a season of highs as City finished ninth and beat Manchester United in the iconic final Manchester derby at Maine Road, Sun was as reliable as they come.

In doing so, he became a cult hero on the Kippax.

Keegan’s sides always attacked relentlessly, sometimes sacrificing defensive solidity.

While that allowed the likes of Nicolas Anelka, Shaun Wright-Phillips and Ali Benarbia to fully express themselves, it could prove difficult for the back line.

With Niclas Jensen on the left and Sylvain Distin, Richard Dunne and Steve Howey featuring in the middle, City were well stocked in defence. Although, given our style of play, they would have to be brave and bold.

Although not an overly imposing figure, especially by the measures of English football at the time, Sun was by no means second best in physical battles.

Very much in the mould of full-backs that have gone on to make the biggest mark at the Club, Sun was always willing to put his body on the line.

He also had the composure and close control that made him an effective player further up the pitch, and even sometimes saw him given responsibility to fill in in midfield.

After winning our Player of the Month vote in September 2002, he became the first East Asian player to score in the Premier League when he headed home the first in a 2-0 win over Birmingham City.

A key part of the buildup, Sun brought the ball in from the right and laid it off to Marc-Vivien Foe. The Cameroonian midfielder swung it out wide to Eyal Berkovic, who crossed directly on to Sun’s head on the penalty spot.

It typified both Sun’s determination but also his willingness and license to push forward into unconventional positions for a full-back.

He played even more in 2003/04, City’s first year at the Etihad Stadium, making 42 appearances including five of our six matches in the UEFA Cup.

We found the going tougher that season, but Sun was at his best and was vital in keeping Keegan’s team afloat in the top tier.

A serious injury early in the 2004/05 campaign curtailed that season, meaning he played just seven times.

2005/06 saw a return to fitness and his second most productive season in sky blue, featuring 34 times across all competitions.

Now managed by Stuart Pearce, City were a different side to the one we had been under Kevin Keegan. Although goals were much harder to come by, it was our defence that ensured our continued existence in the Premier League.

We conceded just 48 times across 38 games on our way to a 15th place finish, a better defensive record than anyone else outside of the top eight.

Richard Dunne was the Club’s Player of the Year, but able assistance from Sun amongst others was crucial in that.

The China international remained at City for two more seasons, although his involvement lessened in Pearce’s second year and once Sven-Goran Eriksson took charge.

After six years and 151 appearances, Sun moved on to Sheffield United in the summer of 2008. He stayed there for one season before returning to China, playing until 2016.

Perhaps the most successful Chinese footballing export, Sun made just as much a mark in Manchester as he did in China, without attracting much attention from the rest of the league.

Since retiring, he has headed up the sports data company HaiQui Sports.

We start in Hong Kong against Italian champions Inter on Saturday 1 August, with kick-off at 12:30 (UK).

After that, we go to Seoul to play a K League All-Stars side on Wednesday 5 August and then Atletico Madrid on Sunday 9 August. Both games begin at 12:00 (UK).

All three will be available to watch on mancity.com and our official app for current CITY+ subscribers globally*.

Available for £34.99 for an annual subscription or £9.99 per month, CITY+ gets you closer to the Club than ever before, with documentaries, live games and full-match replays throughout the season.

*City v Inter: Globally available except China, Italy

K League All-Stars v City: Globally available except South Korea

City v Atletico Madrid: Globally available except South Korea, Spain

title: Bello Shines in Bulk as Red Sox Streak Ends
description: The Red Sox lost their 15-game streak, but Brayan Bello's dominant relief outing suggests a new role for the stretch run.
fullText:
Our surging Red Sox finally saw their 15-game win streak end. That total sits just one game shy of the franchise record set in 1946. Fans poured into Fenway Park expecting history, but the day-night doubleheader had other plans. Boston crushed the Orioles 6-3 in Game 1 to tie that historic mark. Then came the 5-1 defeat in Game 2 to break the run. This marks their first loss since July 1 against the Nationals. The mood shifted instantly from celebration to reality for the Boston faithful.

The joy of tying the 1946 record evaporated in a single inning of Game 2.

Lefty Eduardo Rivera gave up four runs in the first inning of Game 2. He only recorded two outs before the damage was done. That early blow killed any chance of a 16th straight win. Brayan Bello stepped in to save the day with a massive relief effort. He pitched 5 1/3 scoreless frames to keep the game within reach. Bello allowed just four hits while walking only one batter. He struck out five Orioles during his dominant appearance. This performance follows a rough outing against Baltimore on June 4. He was optioned to Triple-A Worcester after that loss. He returned on the Sunday before the All-Star break due to injuries. Two starters, Connelly Early and Ranger Suarez, sit on the 15-day injured list. Bello topped out at 98.4 mph on his sinker during this game. He averaged 96.5 mph, which is well above his season average. His season average sits at 94.9 mph. Bello said he felt rested and fresh. He believes that feeling explains why his velocity was there. This success comes after he followed an opener this season. He posted a 0.77 ERA in six outings while in that role. It is clear he thrives when used in this specific capacity.

He was optioned to Triple-A Worcester just weeks ago, yet he returned with velocity that dwarfed his season norms.

The Red Sox must keep Brayan Bello in this bulk relief role immediately. Sending him back to Worcester would be a massive mistake for the team. His velocity numbers prove he is not tired or struggling physically. He throws harder than ever when he enters the game with a fresh arm. The stats support the idea that he is an elite option out of the pen. A 0.77 ERA in six outings is not a fluke. It shows he has mastered this specific style of pitching. The team has depth issues with Early and Suarez on the shelf. Bello provides the stability they desperately need for the stretch run. He is the best solution for the middle innings right now. Managers should not waste this talent on a minor league assignment. The front office must trust the numbers and the performance. Keeping him here gives Boston the best shot at winning games. His velocity spike suggests he is ready to handle heavy workloads. This is the moment to lock him into the rotation of the bullpen.

With Early and Suarez sidelined, sending Bello down feels like a self-inflicted wound on our playoff hopes.

Fans should watch how the team utilizes Bello in the coming weeks. If they send him down, they ignore the clear evidence of his success. The team faces Toronto this weekend at Fenway Park. Expect Bello to be a key part of the plan again. His role could define the rest of the regular season. The organization must decide if they want to win now. Using Bello as a starter or a long reliever might be the answer. The path to the playoffs looks brighter with him on the mound. Do not be surprised if he stays in Boston for the long haul.

Toronto awaits this weekend. Can we afford to bench our best weapon?

The playoff path looks a whole lot brighter with him on the mound.

title: Barcelona submit counter-proposal to unblock Marc-Andre ter Stegen’s Ajax loan
description: Barcelona are working to get Marc-Andre ter Stegen’s loan move to Ajax back on course after an unexpected financial complication threatened to derail what had appeared to be a straightforward agreem...
fullText:
Barcelona are working to get Marc-Andre ter Stegen’s loan move to Ajax back on course after an unexpected financial complication threatened to derail what had appeared to be a straightforward agreement.

According to Mundo Deportivo, the Catalan club has presented a new proposal to the German goalkeeper after unresolved tax obligations delayed the approval of the transfer.

The latest Barcelona transfer news suggests Ter Stegen is now viewing the revised offer positively, increasing optimism that the deal can finally move towards completion.

The transfer had been expected to progress smoothly after Barcelona, Ajax and the player reached broad agreements earlier in the summer.

However, unexpected issues surrounding the structure of the goalkeeper’s salary temporarily brought negotiations to a standstill.

The report states that the delay came from Ter Stegen’s concerns regarding the tax implications of certain components within his salary package for the season.

Barcelona are eager to finalise Ter Stegen’s exit. (Photo by Alex Caparros/Getty Images)

Barcelona have since intervened by presenting a counter-proposal that adjusts parts of the financial structure in an effort to help resolve the situation.

The goalkeeper is reportedly considering the revised terms favourably, opening the door for the transfer.

Although the deal is advancing, the outlet assures that Ajax will not assume a significant portion of the goalkeeper’s wages.

The Dutch club are expected to contribute less than €2 million towards his salary, with Barcelona covering the majority of his earnings under a contract that runs until 2028.

Importantly, the amount Barcelona pay will not be fixed. Instead, it will vary depending on Ter Stegen’s playing time during the loan spell, a condition Ajax requested after previous experiences with loan agreements.

What initially appeared to be a routine loan has developed into one of the more complicated Barcelona transfer stories of the summer.

Ter Stegen’s loan move to Ajax is back on track. (Photo by Alex Caparros/Getty Images)

Last week, Barcelona publicly indicated that the remaining obstacle rested entirely with the player and his personal financial obligations.

However, recognising that continued delays would benefit nobody, the club decided to become more actively involved by restructuring certain financial details to help facilitate the move.

Ajax have been surprised by the prolonged negotiations, believing that the principal terms had already been agreed and that only the formal documentation remained.

While the Eredivisie side have not imposed a strict deadline, they are also aware that the situation cannot remain unresolved indefinitely.

Club officials are therefore preparing for multiple outcomes, including the possibility that the loan proceeds as expected or that it ultimately collapses.

title: Arsenal explore Yan Diomande deal after Chelsea land Rogers, with PSG ahead and Leipzig holding out on fee
description: Arsenal have explored a deal to sign Yan Diomande from RB Leipzig, after missing out on the signing of Morgan Rogers.The North London side have made initial enquiries to discuss the demands of a deal,...
fullText:
Arsenal have explored a deal to sign Yan Diomande from RB Leipzig, after missing out on the signing of Morgan Rogers.

The North London side have made initial enquiries to discuss the demands of a deal, following Rogers' transfer to Chelsea.

Liverpool had an offer of around £85m turned down for Diomande earlier this summer, with Leipzig keen to retain the winger for another season. The Reds have since cooled their interest, after Diomande declared his preference for Paris Saint-Germain.

The Ivory Coast international has made it clear that he favours a move to France, though PSG are yet to reach an agreement with Leipzig over a fee. Diomande has agreed personal terms with PSG ahead of a potential move.

According totalkSPORT, Arsenal are exploring the conditions of a transfer as the Gunners seek an alternative to Rogers.

Diomande's ability to operate on either side appeals to Arsenal, who are in the process of overhauling their frontline. Christos Tzolis is set to arrive from Club Brugge, while Leandro Trossard has left for Besiktas. Gabriel Martinelli's future at the Emirates is also in doubt.

Diomande has emerged as one of the most in-demand talents in European football after a superb season in theBundesliga.

The 19-year-old scored 12 goals and added eight assists, earning the Bundesliga's Rookie of the Season award. He then impressed at this summer's World Cup with the Ivory Coast, who reached the knockout stages for the first time.

title: Football fans believe Inter Miami exposed Lionel Messi’s reason for crying after FIFA World Cup
description: After seeing Inter Miami’s blunder against the Chicago Fire, many fans are convinced that Lionel Messi isn’t going to be happy to return to the team. Messi is set to miss some time from Inter Miami as he recovers and rests following a gruelling FIFA World Cup campaign.
fullText:
After seeing Inter Miami’s blunder against the Chicago Fire, many fans are convinced that Lionel Messi isn’t going to be happy to return to the team.

Messi is set to miss some time from Inter Miami as he recovers and rests following a gruelling FIFA World Cup campaign.

But after seeing Rocco Novo, the Argentine goalkeeper for Inter Miami’s mistake against the Chicago Fire, he might want to extend his time away from the team.

During Inter Miami’s game against the Chicago Fire, Rocco Novo asked Facundo Mura to pass him the ball as the Fire players continued to press high up the field.

However, as he tried to clear the ball, Novo completely mistimed his kick, and it resulted in an own-goal that gave the Fire a 1-0 lead.

As soon as the clip of this incident emerged on X, it went viral as many fans remarked on the level of play in the MLS, less than a week after one of the best FIFA World Cup campaigns came to an end.

One fan wrote, “Messi wasn’t crying because he lost the World Cup, he was crying because he knew he had to go back to this.”

Another fan continued the joke, adding, “Is he just playing in MLS for s—- and giggles or what?”

A third fan used this as an opportunity to praise Messi, noting, “Dawg, Messi has been carrying s— players all his life!”

“You mean go back to this freedom? Weak league, everybody gets goals and assists, defenders keep making mistakes,” wrote a fourth fan who suggested Messi enjoys the seemingly low level of play in the MLS.

A fifth fan remarked, “Bro won the World Cup and 8 Ballon d’Ors just to play against Chicago Fire in 95°F heat on a Tuesday afternoon.”

And one final fan lamented Messi not playing in the top leagues in Europe, saying, “Such a shame, man, should’ve been in Europe this whole time.”

Coming into the tournament, few could have predicted Lionel Messi to have the kind of campaign that he did with Argentina.

Not only did he help lead Argentina back to the World Cup final, but he also did so while still being the best player on the team by a comfortable margin at 39 years of age.

Many people had presumed that Messi would be a diminished version of himself at this age and wouldn’t deliver the performances we saw from him in his prime.

But the magician from Rosario managed to bag eight goals and four assists in the tournament. And even though his performance in the final against Spain was unremarkable, it doesn’t take away from his stellar campaign.

Many fans believe that Messi reminded the world that, even at his age and after playing in the MLS, he is still one of the best players in the world and could easily thrive at the highest level.

Perhaps he finds an opportunity to return to FC Barcelona before he ends his career, as such a legendary chapter of his career ending on such a sour note won’t sit right with him in the long run.

description: The Dayton Dragons won thrillingly for the second straight night.
fullText:

title: Donald Trump set for further scrutiny as new FIFA complaint to be lodged after the World Cup
description: The World Cup came to an end at the weekend, but the controversy surrounding the President of the United States and Folarin Balogun’s suspension U-turn is not going away any time soon. Mauricio Pochettino’s men have not kicked a ball in weeks having been knocked out of the World Cup in the last 16 by Belgium.
fullText:
The World Cup came to an end at the weekend, but the controversy surrounding the President of the United States and Folarin Balogun’s suspension U-turn is not going away any time soon.

Mauricio Pochettino’s men have not kicked a ball in weeks having been knocked out of the World Cup in the last 16 by Belgium.

However, their legacy from this summer’s competition has been tarnished following what was a promising summer for the USMNT.

The saga surrounding Balogun’s sudden availability to play in that game against Belgium has not gone down well in the world of soccer.

Trump was involved in the process that led FIFA to make a U-turn on the striker’s red card suspension, causing many people to question the morality of the decision.

Although the USMNT did not benefit from the debacle, there are nations like Norway that do not what to see anything like this ever happen again.

Trump admitted he called Gianni Infantino regarding Balogun’s red card, with the president suggesting the suspension should be overturned.

It was a completely unprecedented situation that took the world of soccer by shock, but FIFA are now being urged to admit the decision was wrong.

A report from The Times has claimed that Lise Klaveness, the president of the Norwegian Football Federation, wants an answer from Infantino.

The Norwegian soccer governing body has admitted they do not want to see this controversy simply swept “under the carpet”.

Although the saga likely hindered the USMNT heading into the game in Seattle, it is something that soccer fans do not want to see anything like this ever happen again.

There are serious concerns that political neutrality was breached given the close relationship Trump and Infantino have seemingly formed.

In recent days, it has been suggested that Trump has suggested Infantino is given a role in the United Nations.

Clearly there is a feeling that the relationship between Trump and the FIFA president compromised the ethics of the World Cup.

What will come of Norway’s protest will certainly be interesting.

title: Grandad Porro, Pep’s doubts and 5️⃣ stories to start the day
description: There you have your coffee and toast ready; we’ll take care of the rest with five perfect football stories to kick off Thursday.🥇 Mission accomplished, GrandpaPedro Porro gives his grandfather th...
fullText:
There you have your coffee and toast ready; we’ll take care of the rest with five perfect football stories to kick off Thursday.

Pedro Porro gives his grandfather the world champion’s medal and he breaks down in tears: “This is such a source of pride, you have no idea. Mission accomplished.”

If Pedro Porro’s grandfather — an absolute icon for his comments during the World Cup — is happy, then so are we.

According to Gazzetta dello Sport, the Spanish coach is asking for €20 million per season to manage Italy, but the FIGC’s offer is currently around €10 million.

On top of that, Guardiola has promised his family a break, so a deal still seems a long way off. Maldini and Leonardo are working from within the federation to convince the former Manchester City boss.

Waiting in the federation’s backup plan, if Pep doesn’t work out, are familiar names like Conte and Mancini.

Antoine opened his MLS account with a classy goal for Orlando City in the 0-4 win over the San Jose Earthquakes. It was his 300th club goal.

Jamie Vardy has revealed that N'Golo Kanté was set on representing Mali at international level, but he and other Leicester players convinced him to play for France instead: “He wanted to represent Mali to honour his father’s roots. We convinced him he’d win the World Cup with France.” Good job.

This article was translated into English by Artificial Intelligence. You can read the original version in 🇪🇸 here.

title: Georges St-Pierre Reveals His Mindset Before Final Round Against Johny Hendricks
description: Georges St-Pierre revealed his mindset during the final round of his welterweight title fight against Johny Hendricks at UFC 167. The post Georges St-Pierre Reveals His Mindset Before Final Round Against Johny Hendricks appeared first on HEAVY.
fullText:
Georges St-Pierre revealed his mindset during the final round of his welterweight title fight against Johny Hendricks. St-Pierre earned a unanimous decision in what became his final welterweight title defense.

‘Rush’ absorbed significant damage during the fight. Hendricks, known for his power, controlled several moments during the bout. However, St-Pierre remained resilient and did enough to win the fight on the scorecards.

More than a decade after UFC 167, St-Pierre revealed what he was thinking before the 5th round. The Canadian admitted that he has accepted the possibility that Hendricks was going to win.

“There’s a moment right before the last round of that fight, I’m sitting on the stool and it was a very tough fight, very close,” St-Pierre said on his GSP Podcast. “My coach is telling me, ‘Georges, it’s 2-2. This is the final round. It’s do or die.'”

“I’m sweaty, I’m busted up, I have a bunch of marks on my face. I’m looking across, I’m looking at [Hendricks] and I’m thinking, ‘It’s okay. It happens sometimes. He’s younger, he’s more motivated, he probably wants it more than me.’ By the way, I don’t even want to be there.'”

Georges St-Pierre also credited Royce Gracie for giving him added motivation to continue and shift momentum back into his favor. During Gracie’s appearance on St-Pierre’s GSP Podcast, the Canadian said the Brazilian’s presence gave him a spark.

“I’m having this thought that comes back to me. I’m like, ‘How about Royce? Royce is watching you. Arnold Schwarzenegger was in the crowd as well and he’s watching you,'” he said. “Then I’m like, ‘[Expletive] that. I can’t lose this fight in front of my idol.’ Then it pumped me up again. I finished strong in the 5th round and I won the fight [by] decision.”

St-Pierre spent time with Gracie during fight week and did not want to deliver a poor performance in front of him.

St-Pierre also revealed that he was dealing with depression at the time of his fight with Johny Hendricks at UFC 167. He faced the pressure associated with being one of the UFC‘s biggest stars and trying to balance his personal life.

St-Pierre expressed gratitude for Gracie spending time with him before his fight and for inspiring him to pursue MMA. ‘Rush’ said he did not think he would have found the motivation to continue without the UFC Hall of Famer there.

“Thank you. Because if you would not have been there, man, I think I would not have the energy, the motivation to do it. I was in a dark time,” St-Pierre said during GSP Podcast. “Then I took time off. I needed to get out because I was in a little depression at that time and I was too embarrassed to admit it, because I was thinking if I talk about it, the fans would say, ‘What does he have to complain about? He’s rich, he’s popular.'”

He added: “If I was able to do it and finish that chapter of my life well, it’s because of you. Man, you’re an inspiration, Royce.”

Like HEAVY's content? Be sure to follow us.

This article was originally published on HEAVY

title: Chesters signs for Wealdstone
description: Wealdstone sign attacking midfielder Dan Chesters on a permanent deal after he spent the past two seasons at Salford City.
fullText:
Wealdstone have signed attacking midfielder Dan Chesters on a permanent deal after he spent the past two seasons at Salford City.

The 24-year-old is a product of West Ham's academy and made his senior debut for the Hammers in the Europa League.

"Dan is an intelligent attacking player who is comfortable on the ball and has the ability to create chances in the final third," manager Gary Waddock said.

"There was interest in him from elsewhere, so we are delighted that he has chosen to join us. He has settled into the squad quickly and we believe he will be an exciting addition to the team."

title: The Chelsea-Man City link
description: Chelsea not only lost to Manchester City in the 2020 FA Youth Cup final, they also had a glimpse of their own future within the opposition ranks.  The latest addition is club-record £117m signing Morgan Rogers, who scored against the Blues' under-18s that night six years ago.  Fellow City academy graduate Cole Palmer was also on target in City's 3-2 victory at St George's Park, while Liam Delap led the line.
fullText:
Chelsea not only lost to Manchester City in the 2020 FA Youth Cup final, they also had a glimpse of their own future within the opposition ranks.

So much so that Chelsea's first-team squad in the 2026-27 season could contain twice as many players who have graduated from City's academy as have come through their own system.

The latest addition is club-record £117m signing Morgan Rogers, who scored against the Blues' under-18s that night six years ago.

Fellow City academy graduate Cole Palmer was also on target in City's 3-2 victory at St George's Park, while Liam Delap led the line.

In addition, Xabi Alonso's squad features Romeo Lavia, Jamie Gittens and Tosin Adarabioyo - all of whom also came through City's youth system.

Together with Rogers, Palmer and Delap, the six players account for almost £300m in transfer fees paid by Chelsea since 2023.

Trevoh Chalobah also remains a Chelsea player, but he is in advanced talks over a move to Serie A side Como.

There are several reasons for Chelsea's interest in players developed from Manchester City, but one is the success of Palmer. Having moved between the clubs in 2023 for £40m plus £2.5m in add-ons, he has become one of the Premier League's standout performers.

One former insider told BBC Sport that anyone who comes from City "knows what good looks like".

Another factor is Joe Shields, who worked in City's academy recruitment department and is now involved in both youth and senior recruitment at Stamford Bridge.

Chelsea also employ a number of former City staff members, including first-team coach Calum McFarlane and recently promoted academy director Glenn van der Kraan.

There was also a brief spell for former City executive Tom Glick as Chelsea's president of business, while former Blues head coach Enzo Maresca and several members of his coaching staff previously worked at the Etihad.

Chelsea also held an interest in former City academy player Michael Olise before his move to Bayern Munich and previously explored a deal for defender Nico O'Reilly.

title: India's biggest weakness ahead of Zimbabwe T20I series: How the Chevrons can exploit it
description: India struggled against hard-length pace in Ireland and England. Can Zimbabwe's Blessing Muzarabani and Richard Ngarava exploit that weakness in the T20I series?
fullText:
India's biggest weakness ahead of Zimbabwe T20I series: How the Chevrons can exploit it originally appeared on Cricket News. Add Cricket News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

India struggled against hard-length pace bowling during the T20I tours against Ireland and England, in which they failed to win a single game.

Zimbabwe's pace attack, led by Blessing Muzarabani and Richard Ngarava could exploit India in the same manner.

The Chevrons currently possess one of the most improved home seam attacks in world cricket.

Conditions in Harare could once again test India's batting against back-of-a-length bowling.

In a normal world, back-to-back T20 World Cup champions India would enter the three-match T20I series against Zimbabwe as clear favourites. However, after failing to win a single match across seven T20Is in Ireland and England, their aura has taken a significant hit.

New captain Shreyas Iyer is already under pressure to get India's white-ball rebuild back on track. While India will still start as favourites, Zimbabwe is no longer a side that the visitors can take lightly, especially if they replicate the blueprint that repeatedly exposed India's batters during the UK tour.

The Chevrons possess the weapon that could make life uncomfortable for the visitors.

Throughout the tours of Ireland and England, India's batters struggled against disciplined hard-length bowling. Pace bowlers who consistently hit the deck and extracted steep bounce found success, particularly on grounds with longer boundaries where mistimed pulls and cuts rarely carried to the fence.

Blessing Muzarabani and Richard Ngarava have spearheaded Zimbabwe's fast-bowling resurgence.

🇿🇼 🔥#ZIMvINDpic.twitter.com/0IgFp1Rj45

— Cricbuzz (@cricbuzz) July 23, 2026

Zimbabwe's pace attack, spearheaded by their premier fast bowlers Blessing Muzarabani and Richard Ngarava, is arguably better equipped than ever to exploit that exact weakness.

Since the start of 2025, Zimbabwe's seam attack has ranked among the best of the Full Member nations at home despite the team sitting near the bottom of the ICC rankings. Muzarabani and Ngarava have led that rise, combining for nearly 100 wickets as an opening pair in T20Is.

Standing well over six feet tall with naturally high release points, both bowlers relentlessly attack the uncomfortable 8-10 metre hard-length area before mixing in steep bouncers that rush batters for time.

It is precisely the formula that troubled India throughout their recent white-ball tours.

MORE:Under the microscope: Why India's T20I Zimbabwe tour is no longer a formality for Shreyas Iyer and Co.

India's batting numbers from the Ireland and England T20Is reveal a worrying pattern.

According to Cricbuzz, India lost 13 wickets at an average below 15 against deliveries pitched on a hard length (8-10 metres) during the 2026 UK tour.

Another seven dismissals came through short-pitched deliveries, which may have exposed India's inability to consistently dominate bowlers who refused to pitch the ball up.

Those struggles were without a doubt amplified by the larger boundaries in the UK, where mishits that routinely sailed over the ropes during IPL 2026 instead found fielders stationed within the boundary lines.

Harare, though, is not the same as the UK stadiums but can offer a similar test. The venue has generally produced lower-scoring contests than the batting-friendly surfaces seen during IPL 2026, where 200-plus totals became a routine story.

Wickets in the Powerplay! 👌

A strong start from our bowlers.

Match Centre: https://t.co/y99hHk4MbWpic.twitter.com/CEuKabu6MQ

— England Cricket (@englandcricket) July 9, 2026

If India once again struggles to handle steep bounce and relentless back-of-a-length bowling, Zimbabwe have the ideal pace attack to capitalise. It could prove to be the hosts' biggest weapon against an Indian batting line-up still searching for answers.

That said, Zimbabwe's bowling threat isn't limited to pace alone. Veteran all-rounder Sikandar Raza remains the heartbeat of the side and could play a crucial role with his off-spin against India's left-handed-heavy top order.

With Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Abhishek Sharma expected to open the batting, don't be surprised if Raza introduces himself inside the Powerplay to exploit the favourable matchup before handing the attack back to his two fast-bowling spearheads.

For all the latest cricket news, opinion and commentary and to share your voice head to our Facebook, Instagram and X (Twitter) pages.

title: ‘Putting Others in Danger’ — Columnist Argues Caitlin Clark’s Flopping Is Dangerous Due to Her ‘White Nationalist’ Fans
description: Any way you slice it, the WNBA has a Caitlin Clark problem on its hands, and the list of concerns seems to be growing by the day. First came complaints about a lack of protection for the former No.
fullText:
Any way you slice it, the WNBA has a Caitlin Clark problem on its hands, and the list of concerns seems to be growing by the day.

First came complaints about a lack of protection for the former No. 1 overall pick. Then came criticism that the league had become overly physical, with some arguing that Clark still wasn’t getting the benefit of the whistle. Now, one journalist claims Clark may have overadjusted her game to the point of flopping, potentially endangering other players.

Interest in the WNBA is increasing, which is a great development for the sport. It’s leading to more quality discussions about how to further grow the game.

It’s also leading to a wider range of opinions.

On Wednesday, USA Today’s Nancy Armour went so far as to assert that Clark’s tendency to flop is placing other WNBA players at risk. It’s a passionate rant, one that’s sure to ruffle feathers.

“Indiana Fever All-Star Caitlin Clark and her enablers must take the rest of us for idiots,” Armour wrote. “The fouls they claim occurred? The ‘contusion’ she says she suffered? The technicals that aren’t deserved? Clark doesn’t suddenly become invisible when she’s involved in a physical play, so we can see what actually happened. Which is that Clark is flopping. And her histrionics while doing so are putting other people in danger.”

MORE: ‘UNSTOPPABLE’ — WNBA World Reacts to Caitlin Clark’s ‘Lights-Out’ Performance As Fever Set Franchise Scoring Record

Then, Armour took things up a notch, bringing race into the picture.

“It seems there is a white nationalist element to Clark’s fandom that is deeply, deeply problematic, made even more so because the WNBA is made up predominantly of Black women, many of them queer,” Armour added.

“When Clark claims fouls that don’t exist, when she goes on profanity-laced tirades at referees, those racists see her as a white woman in need of saving and take matters into their own hands.”

The columnist used the location of the WNBA’s upcoming All-Star Game as an example to help further her case, continuing to point the finger partially at Clark for an apparent lack of awareness.

“It shouldn’t need reminding this country has an awful history of Black people being harmed, even killed, in the name of ‘defending’ white women,” Armour continued.

“The WNBA’s All-Star Game this weekend is in Chicago, Emmett Till’s hometown, and a local art gallery is currently hosting an exhibit imagining his life if he hadn’t been lynched. While it’s incumbent on the WNBA to do more to protect its players, there also should be an expectation on Clark to recognize the wider consequences of her actions.”

Overall, Armour claimed she’s “not a Clark hater,” acknowledging that the Fever guard is “great for the league and women’s sports.” Still, Armour contends that Clark will be “even better when she cuts out the Oscar auditions.”

title: Al Ittihad submit bid for Eintracht Frankfurt target Raphael Onyedika
description: Al Ittihad have submitted an official bid to Club Brugge for Raphael Onyedika after Eintracht Frankfurt made their opening approach on Wednesday, according to a report by Fabrizio Romano.It comes less...
fullText:
Al Ittihad have submitted an official bid to Club Brugge for Raphael Onyedika after Eintracht Frankfurt made their opening approach on Wednesday, according to a report by Fabrizio Romano.

It comes less than one week after Frankfurt reached a verbal agreement to sign the Nigerian midfielder – who, with 12 months remaining on his current deal, is valued at around €10m by his current employers.

As Frankfurt gear up for 2026/27 under returning head coach Adi Hütter, they’ve already secured the services of German youth international Noël Aséko from Bayern Munich for €13m.

The Hessen club have a surplus in transfer revenue following the high profile departures of Nathaniel Brown (Bayern Munich, €50m), Aurèle Amenda (Coventry City, €18m) and Rasmus Kristensen (Midtjylland, €6m), while re-investing that in the aforementioned Aséko, Malik Pimpong (Midtjylland, €3.5m) and the returning Noel Futkeu (Greuther Fürth, €1.3m), who is expected to leave the club.

title: Mbappe makes personal plea for Real Madrid to sign his French teammate
description: Real Madrid’s long-running links with Michael Olise have gathered fresh momentum after a new report claimed Kylian Mbappe has personally informed Florentino Perez that the Bayern Munich winger is ea...
fullText:
Real Madrid’s long-running links with Michael Olise have gathered fresh momentum after a new report claimed Kylian Mbappe has personally informed Florentino Perez that the Bayern Munich winger is eager to join the Spanish giants.

According to Mundo Deportivo, Mbappe contacted the Real Madrid president following France’s defeat to Spain in the FIFA World Cup semi-finals to relay Olise’s interest in making the move to the Santiago Bernabeu.

While Real Madrid’s transfer plans regarding Olise have yet to progress into formal negotiations, the aforementioned development has placed the French winger at the centre of attention once again.

Despite previous public denials from the club regarding any interest in Olise, reports continue to suggest that the 24-year-old sees his future in Madrid.

As per the outlet, Mbappe has become one of the strongest advocates for Michael Olise’s move to Real Madrid.

The two France internationals have developed a strong understanding on the international stage, and the Real Madrid forward has encouraged his teammate to make the switch to Spain.

Mbappe has informed Florentino Perez that Olise wants to move to Real Madrid. (Photo by Al Bello/Getty Images)

Mbappe is said to believe Olise would fit naturally into Jose Mourinho’s project and told him that the club both wants and needs his qualities in attack.

In fact, following France’s World Cup exit, Mbappe informed Perez that Olise is enthusiastic about the prospect of playing at the Bernabeu.

Those claims follow earlier reports, which stated that Olise had already shared his desire to play for Real Madrid with several of his France teammates.

While Olise’s interest has fuelled excitement, the biggest obstacle remains Bayern Munich’s position.

Earlier in the summer, Real Madrid issued an official statement dismissing rumours linking the club with the winger, a move viewed as an attempt to avoid creating unnecessary tension with the Bundesliga champions.

Mbappe could play a key role in Olise’s transfer. (Photo by Molly Darlington/Getty Images)

Bayern have also maintained that they have no intention of opening negotiations for one of their most important attacking players.

Even so, speculation surrounding the Michael Olise transfer has refused to disappear.

It is said that the winger believes he would be closer to achieving his ambition of winning the UEFA Champions League by joining Real Madrid.

In fact, Olise feels his cycle at Bayern may already be nearing its conclusion after two seasons in Germany.

At present, however, there have been no direct discussions between the two clubs, meaning any potential move remains at an early stage despite the growing volume of reports.

title: Little margin for error in Newcastle's bid to be a top club by 2030
description: As the fifth anniversary of Newcastle United's Saudi-led takeover approaches in October, the club clearly has work to do on and off the field.  Chairman Yasir Al-Rumayyan set out to be "number one", while chief executive David Hopkinson's aim was to be "in the debate about being the top club in the world" by 2030 - but Newcastle need the infrastructure to match.  An announcement is awaited on plans for a new state-of-the-art training ground after a site was identified in Woolsington near Newcastle Airport.
fullText:
As the fifth anniversary of Newcastle United's Saudi-led takeover approaches in October, the club clearly has work to do on and off the field.

Chairman Yasir Al-Rumayyan set out to be "number one", while chief executive David Hopkinson's aim was to be "in the debate about being the top club in the world" by 2030 - but Newcastle need the infrastructure to match.

An announcement is awaited on plans for a new state-of-the-art training ground after a site was identified in Woolsington near Newcastle Airport.

The club have stressed the importance of taking time to make the right decision when it comes to either expanding St James' Park or building a new stadium, which will be key to boosting revenue.

To consistently compete, however, there is little margin for error.

Newcastle needed to see more of an immediate return from their £100m-plus net spend last summer, for example, but defender Malick Thiaw has been the only unquestioned success.

The club are also counting the cost of a bruising 12th-placed finish last season after missing out on Europe.

Most pertinently, after breaching Uefa's financial sustainability regulations, Newcastle have entered into a three-year settlement with European football's governing body.

Raising funds through the sales of Anthony Gordon and Sandro Tonali to Barcelona and Tottenham respectively was therefore crucial in order for the club to have the ability to reinvest this summer.

Three key players have now left in less than 12 months, though, which has weakened the side considerably, following Alexander Isak's painful £125m departure to Liverpool last summer.

Newcastle do not want to lose another big name in Bruno Guimaraes, but the captain's future is uncertain after he intimated he wished to join Arsenal.

Whatever happens, trading will remain a key part of the club's strategy in the coming years - and there has been a shift in approach with incomings too.

Further reinforcements are sorely needed but all of the club's arrivals this summer - goalkeeper Ewen Jaouen, midfielder Sean Steur and winger Bazoumana Toure - have been aged 20 and under, and from the continent.

The trio have immense potential and in the right hands could flourish, but they will need time.

It feels like the same could be said for the project as a whole right now.

title: Ben Rice reemerges in Hitter Power Rankings after June slump
description: Yankees rookie Ben Rice bounces back in July to reenter the Hitter Power Rankings, trailing Yordan Alvarez in Triple Crown chase.
fullText:
Ben Rice is back where he belongs. The Yankees rookie just reemerged in the latest Hitter Power Rankings. He dropped out after a rough June but has rallied hard in July. This comeback proves he can handle major league pressure. Fans should lock in their tickets for the best games now.

It is hard to stay on top of these rankings for long. Even great hitters slump at times. Rice is showing no signs of slowing down now. He might be on his way to making some serious history. The panel of MLB.com voters decided these latest rankings.

Rice was a regular in the rankings for much of the season. He dropped out after a tough June that saw a .657 OPS. However, he has rallied in July to get back on the list. His performance is now close to the league leaders. The stats show he is a serious threat to the top spot.

Yordan Alvarez currently holds the number one spot by a unanimous decision. Alvarez is the leader in the AL batting leaderboards. His .328 average is 20 points ahead of the Rays’ Yandy Díaz. His 34 home runs are four ahead of the Yankees’ Ben Rice. His 77 RBIs are six ahead of Rice per reports. The Triple Crown watch is very much on in Houston.

Junior Caminero is another big threat to Alvarez in these rankings. Caminero ranks in the 100th percentile of Major League hitters in bat speed. He also ranks in the 93rd percentile in hard-hit rate. That is no surprise to anyone who watched him in the Home Run Derby. James Wood also ranks in the 100th percentile in average exit velocity. Wood frequently makes ideal contact according to the data.

Rice’s return to the rankings is huge for the Yankees. He proved he can bounce back from a bad month. Most rookies struggle to find their rhythm after a slump. Rice found it fast and it shows in the power rankings. He is not just a flash in the pan. He is a legitimate force in the lineup.

The gap between Rice and Alvarez is shrinking fast. Rice is only four home runs behind the leader. He is also only six RBIs behind the top spot. This race is going to be very exciting for fans. The Yankees need this kind of production to win games. Rice must keep this pace up for the rest of the season.

Alvarez is holding strong in the top spot for the fourth straight poll. But Rice is making noise right behind him. The AL batting leaderboards are tight at the top. Rice has shown he belongs in that conversation. He has the power to change the game for New York.

Fans must watch how Rice handles the next few weeks. He needs to maintain this July momentum to stay ranked. The Triple Crown race will be the main story to follow. Alvarez will try to extend his lead over Rice and others. Rice will try to close the gap on the Astros star.

The Yankees must get more consistent production from their rookie. A strong finish could put them in a better playoff position. Rice is proving he is ready for the big stage. Do not expect him to fade away anytime soon. The power rankings will likely see him climb even higher soon. Lock in your tickets to the best games now.

title: DFB denies big meeting called to name Klopp as Germany coach
description: The German Football Federation (DFB) has denied it is holding a major meeting on Thursday which looked set to rubber-stamp Jürgen Klopp as the Germany men's coach. Sources close to the matter had told dpa that the DFB's supervisory board and stakeholders would meet on Thursday.
fullText:
The German Football Federation (DFB) has denied it is holding a major meeting on Thursday which looked set to rubber-stamp Jürgen Klopp as the Germany men's coach.

Sources close to the matter had told dpa that the DFB's supervisory board and stakeholders would meet on Thursday. But the federation has now denied this, saying details still need to be worked on and an invite to such a meeting has not been sent out.

Speculation has been rife that Klopp would be presented on Friday after signing a deal until 2030. That could now be in doubt.

But all the talk from the 59-year-old former Borrusia Dortmund and Liverpool coach, the DFB and his current employer Red Bull has pointed to a deal being done at some point.

Germany need a coach after Julian Nagelsmann was forced to resign following the last-32 exit to Paraguay at the recent World Cup - a third early flop in a row.

title: Commonwealth Games 2026 schedule, start times and how to watch
description: The 10-sport programme, including six Para sports, will include athletics, gymnastics, cycling and swimming
fullText:
Glasgow hosts a slimmed-down version of the Commonwealth Games as athletes from 74 nations and territories compete for gold in the Scottish city.

Glasgow stepped in after the Australian state of Victoria backed out three years ago due to the cost of hosting the Games.

The 10-sport programme, including six Para sports, will feature athletics, gymnastics, cycling and swimming - and will take place across four Glasgow venues.

The 11,000-spectator Scotstoun Stadium will stage the athletics as well as the biggest home star of the Games, new mile world record holder Josh Kerr.

The Games will be shown live across TNT Sports and HBO Max, rather than on the BBC or terrestrial television.

Here is a closer look at the full day-by-day schedule for the Games, including every session for every sport
