title: Why Ryan Coleman-Williams Could Be Alabama Football's Biggest Breakout Player in 2026
description: After a sobering sophomore slump, the former blue-chip recruit is silencing critics through accountability and grit, positioning himself for a dominant resurgence in the Crimson Tide’s high-powered offense.
fullText:
There's something about redemption stories that makes people want to root for them.

Not because they're guaranteed a happy ending, but because they're real. They're relatable.

Every athlete, every coach, and honestly, every person has experienced a season of life that didn't go according to plan. Sometimes those seasons reveal more about someone's character than the moments when everything comes easily.

I believe that's exactly where Ryan Coleman-Williams finds himself heading into the 2026 season.

If Alabama fans only remember what happened last year, they're missing the bigger picture. Sometimes the best players aren't the ones who explode onto the scene and never face adversity. Sometimes they're the ones who get knocked down, quietly go back to work, and come back stronger because of it.

That's why I think Coleman-Williams has a chance to become one of Alabama's biggest stories this fall.

Not because he's trying to prove his critics wrong. But because he's focused on proving something to himself. That's a much more dangerous mindset.

When Coleman-Williams arrived in Tuscaloosa, the expectations couldn't have been much higher. As one of the nation's top recruits, Alabama fans expected him to become another star receiver in a program that's produced some of college football's biggest names. His freshman season only fueled those expectations. The flashes were there. The athleticism was undeniable. Every time he touched the football, it felt like there was an opportunity for something special to happen.

Then came his sophomore season.

Instead of building on that momentum the way many expected, things became much more difficult.

Development isn't always a straight line.

It's easy for fans to look at statistics and assume they tell the entire story, but they rarely do. Behind every season are countless hours in the weight room, film sessions, injuries, adjustments, confidence battles, and lessons that never appear in a box score.

Sometimes a player's greatest growth happens in a season that doesn't look successful from the outside.

Listening to Coleman-Williams during SEC Media Days, it became clear that he's embraced that reality.

There were no excuses. No blame. No frustration. Just accountability.

"My freshman year, everything was fast-paced," Coleman-Williams said. "I did a lot of learning. Just really taking those things that I did right throughout my freshman year and taking the things that I could have did better in my sophomore year and taking those things and just building the best version, the best plan for myself going into this year and just trying to replicate it day in and day out."

That answer stood out to me because it reflects something that's becoming increasingly rare in today's college football landscape.

In an era dominated by NIL, the transfer portal, and constant outside noise, it would have been easy for Coleman-Williams to point fingers or look for a fresh start elsewhere. Instead, he chose to look inward.

That's what great competitors do.

They don't waste time worrying about what happened yesterday. They focus on becoming better tomorrow.

His answer wasn't about reclaiming the player he was as a freshman. It was about becoming an even better version of himself.

There's a difference. One focuses on the past. The other focuses on growth.

And growth is exactly what Alabama head coach Kalen DeBoer says he's witnessed throughout the offseason.

"I think the experiences that you just naturally go through are what have led him to being able to take the next steps,"DeBoer said. "It's easy when it's going well, but when you have some adversity, especially when you're highlighted like he is... you know, we're all human, right? Him being able to stay strong, continue to focus on what he needs to improve on, but what he can control, is just so impressive."

One phrase especially stuck with me.

Coleman-Williams can't control what fans post on social media.

He can't control preseason expectations. He can't control recruiting rankings or comparisons to other receivers. He can't change last season.

What he can control is how hard he works today.

How prepared he is when practice begins. How he treats his teammates. How he responds when adversity comes again.

Championship players understand that difference.

That's one reason I think Alabama fans should be excited about what Coleman-Williams could become this season.

He's no longer chasing expectations. He's chasing consistency.

That word came up repeatedly during his time at SEC Media Days.

When asked about his goals for the upcoming season, Coleman-Williams didn't mention receptions. He didn't mention touchdowns. He didn't mention awards.

Instead, he gave one of my favorite answers from the entire event.

"Honestly, for myself, one of the biggest things that's always been important to me is being a team captain for my squad."

That tells you everything you need to know about his priorities.

In today's game, conversations often revolve around NIL earnings, NFL Draft projections, and individual accolades.

Coleman-Williams talked about leadership.

Being a captain at Alabama isn't something that's handed out because you're talented. It's earned.

Nick Saban always emphasized that leadership comes from consistency, accountability, and doing the right things every single day. While Saban is no longer patrolling the sidelines in Tuscaloosa, that standard hasn't changed.

Coleman-Williams understands that.

"It's like an extra chip on your shoulder just to be able to... be my best self," he said.

He didn't say he wanted to be the leading receiver in the SEC. He didn't say he wanted All-American honors. He didn't say he wanted to hear his name called in the first round of the NFL Draft.

Instead, he said something even more revealing.

"I know the individual accolades will handle themselves."

There's wisdom in that statement. Too many athletes become consumed with chasing awards instead of chasing excellence.

The funny thing is that excellence usually produces the awards anyway. That's exactly what Coleman-Williams seems to understand.

His focus isn't on recognition.

It's on becoming the type of player worthy of recognition. That's a mindset coaches love. It's also a mindset teammates respect.

When reporters asked him what he wanted to prove this season, his answer remained remarkably consistent.

"Honestly, just going into this year, my main focus is just trying to be a consistent guy day in and day out. And with the work that I put in, obviously the results will show for themselves. So really just being the best version of myself day in and day out for my squad and for myself."

It's not the flashiest word in sports. It won't trend on social media. It doesn't create highlight reels. But ask any championship coach what they value most, and consistency will almost always be near the top of the list.

Anyone can have one spectacular game.

Great players perform every week. Every practice. Every meeting. Every workout.

That's where championships are built.

Not on Saturdays alone. But on the hundreds of ordinary days that nobody outside the football building ever sees.

According to DeBoer, Coleman-Williams has embraced those ordinary days better than ever before.

"One thing that he's been able to do this year, it wasn't because he wasn't trying to do a year ago, but between being healthier this year in the offseason, and also understanding his body better, and actually a year older too, he's put on some weight and I think that's helped in a lot of different ways," DeBoer said.

That physical growth is important. College football isn't just about talent. It's about development.

Players arrive on campus with incredible athletic ability, but they spend years learning how to fuel their bodies, recover properly, train more efficiently, and maximize their physical gifts.

DeBoer believes Coleman-Williams has reached another level in that process.

"I think it helps with your confidence, your ability to move, I think it's going to help with his ability out there on the field from a physicality standpoint too."

Confidence matters. Not arrogance. Confidence earned through preparation. Confidence built from countless hours of unseen work.

The version of Ryan Coleman-Williams Alabama fans see this fall may not simply be older.

He may be stronger. More confident. More mature. And perhaps most importantly, more prepared for everything this season demands.

One of the things I appreciated most about DeBoer's comments wasn't even about football.

It was about the person Coleman-Williams has become.

"He's just worked extremely hard. He's been as locked in as you could ever imagine," DeBoer said.

That's high praise coming from a coach who has been around elite athletes throughout his career.

"I hope our people appreciate that about him. There are a lot of eyeballs on him. He signed up for it. He's great with it. He embraces it. He gives everything he has to his teammates. He gives everything he has to people he comes in contact with. I love the guy. I'm so glad he's on our side."

That may be the most telling quote of all. DeBoer wasn't talking about speed. He wasn't talking about route running. He wasn't talking about catches. He was talking about character.

Sometimes fans become so focused on production that they forget coaches spend far more time evaluating who a player is than simply what he does on Saturdays.

From everything we've heard this offseason, Ryan Coleman-Williams has become exactly the type of player every coach wants in the locker room.

And if that's true, don't be surprised if the football follows.

Because more often than not, the biggest breakout seasons belong to players who quietly spent the offseason becoming better when no one was paying attention.

Ryan Coleman-Williams isn't asking Alabama fans to forget last season.

He's asking them to watch what comes next.

And if everything we've heard from him and Kalen DeBoer is any indication, the next chapter just might be his best one yet.

title: Does Chad Tracy have any off-field concerns about Roman Anthony, Marcelo Mayer?
description: Rumors about how Roman Anthony and Marcelo Mayer conduct themselves away from the ballpark have made their rounds on social media. Does Chad Tracy have any concerns about the duo when they’re not at the park?
fullText:
Rumors about how Roman Anthony and Marcelo Mayer conduct themselves away from the ballpark have made their rounds on social media.

They only escalated after Anthony’s rehab was moved to Fort Myers and many wondered if it was due to punishment and not a crowded Red Sox clubhouse.

Does Chad Tracy have any concerns about the duo when they’re not at the park?

“Zero,” the Red Sox interim manager said on WEEI. “Not even remotely on my radar. Not something we’ve even talked about in here. I’ve tried to say here publicly the last couple days. I know that gets a lot of attention. Roman in particular is like, I’ve said it publicly: I want Roman healthy and I want Roman back. Roman’s a great baseball player. He’s a hell of a hitter. And he can help us. But, a hell of a hitter with two hands. Not with one. So, we have to get him healthy.”

Anthony has been dealing with a tricky hand injury since May. The outfielder tore a ligament at the base of his finger during an at-bat and hasn’t begun swinging yet.

“Being in Florida allows him to focus on that and do that and not feel like he’s here at Fenway, where he’s the center of attention, everybody’s going to him, asking all these questions,” Tracy said. “He’s very focused on what he’s trying to do right now, but I have no concern over Roman and Marcelo or where they’re going.”

Tracy added he’s in constant communication with Anthony.

Mayer, meanwhile, is dealing with a stress fracture in his forearm. He was recently cleared to dry swing.

Neither player has a timeline for a return to the lineup. There have been questions about whether Anthony will see game action again this season, but Tracy is optimistic.

“I do believe that,” Tracy said. “I’m not saying that’s going to happen, but I do believe that. Mainly because what’s happened to his hand and what’s limiting him still is not limiting him from doing all the other things. He’s been running. He’s been able to throw. He’s been able to get out and get defense. So, if we get this thing to where it needs to be, it literally is just a matter of, we need a hitting progression, we gotta get him out on a rehab assignment. It’s not like he’s been sitting there completely inactive for two and a half months. That’s not the case.”

title: LA Rams’ Matthew Stafford Falls to NFL MVP for Best QB in Latest Rankings
description: The Los Angeles Rams spent the offseason feeling like Matthew Stafford is one of the NFL’s otherworldly quarterbacks for at least one more season.A new national ranking offered an endorsement that bolstered that belief.FOX Sports’ Henry McKenna ranked all 32 projected starting quarterbacks heading into training camp and placed Stafford at No. 2.Only Buffalo Bills […] The post LA Rams’ Matthew Stafford Falls to NFL MVP for Best QB in Latest Rankings appeared first on HEAVY.
fullText:
The Los Angeles Rams spent the offseason feeling like Matthew Stafford is one of the NFL’s otherworldly quarterbacks for at least one more season.

A new national ranking offered an endorsement that bolstered that belief.

FOX Sports’ Henry McKenna ranked all 32 projected starting quarterbacks heading into training camp and placed Stafford at No. 2.

Only Buffalo Bills quarterback Josh Allen finished above him.

Stafford landed ahead of Joe Burrow, Jordan Love, Justin Herbert, Drake Maye and Patrick Mahomes.

McKenna described the exercise as an early look toward Week 1, with quarterback readiness, supporting casts and track records among the considerations.

The placement gives the Rams another reason to enter training camp with Super Bowl expectations.

Stafford earned the ranking after producing the best statistical season of his 17-year career.

The 38-year-old led the NFL with 4,707 passing yards and 46 touchdown passes while throwing eight interceptions. He also finished second with a 109.2 passer rating, led the league with 236 passing first downs and helped Los Angeles finish 12-5.

His efficiency became historic during the middle of the season.

Stafford became the first quarterback in NFL history to throw at least four touchdown passes without an interception in three consecutive regular-season games. He later set a league record by throwing 28 consecutive touchdown passes without an interception.

Those numbers earned Stafford the first Associated Press NFL MVP of his career and a first Associated Press NFL MVP. The Rams then signed him to a contract extension in May, removing one potential distraction before his 18th season.

The ranking still stopped one place short of putting Stafford at the top.

Allen passed for 3,668 yards with 25 touchdowns and 10 interceptions last season, although his rushing ability continues to give Buffalo another dimension.

FOX Sports nevertheless placed Stafford above several younger quarterbacks carrying their own championship expectations.

That group includes Mahomes, who fell to No. 7 as he works back from a torn ACL, and Burrow, who ranked third after an injury-shortened season.

For Stafford, No. 2 represents another sign that age has done little to weaken his standing around the league.

The regular-season production only tells part of the story.

Stafford completed 22 of 35 passes for 374 yards and three touchdowns in the NFC Championship Game. The Rams still fell 31-27 to the Seattle Seahawks after defensive and special teams mistakes left the offense chasing the game.

According to the Rams, Stafford became the first quarterback to throw for at least 350 yards and three touchdowns without a turnover and lose a playoff start. Puka Nacua caught nine passes for 165 yards and a touchdown, but Los Angeles could not complete its final comeback.

That ending reinforced how close the Rams are to another Super Bowl appearance.

Los Angeles selected Ty Simpson with the No. 13 pick in the 2026 NFL draft as Stafford’s eventual successor. The Rams have not established a timeline for that transition, and Stafford said during organized team activities that his body felt good as he prepared for another season.

Training camp begins July 27 at Loyola Marymount University. The Rams can manage Stafford’s workload while developing the depth behind him, but there is little uncertainty about which quarterback determines the team’s ceiling.

FOX Sports placed Stafford behind one quarterback in the league.

After an MVP season and a narrow NFC Championship Game loss, the Rams enter camp with expectations to match that ranking.

Stafford has already shown he can play at the required level. The next challenge is turning it into one more Super Bowl run.

title: Cowboys Predicted to Sign Controversial Free Agent WR
description: SB Nation's No. 1 prediction for the upcoming season has the Dallas Cowboys signing controversial free agent wide receiver Stefon Diggs. The post Cowboys Predicted to Sign Controversial Free Agent WR appeared first on HEAVY.
fullText:
If we have come to understand 1 thing about the Dallas Cowboys and owner Jerry Jones, it’s that he values headlines to the point where “any publicity is good publicity” might as well be etched in stone at AT&T Stadium.

SB Nation’s Jarrett Bailey’s list of predictions for the upcoming NFL season includes a headline-grabber for the ages in the No. 1 spot, where he predicts the Cowboys will sign controversial NFL All-Pro wide receiver Stefon Diggs.

“I know everyone is metaphorically stuffing Diggs in a box, taping him up, and FedEx-ing him to the Washington Commanders’ facility in Landover, but it’s another NFC East team that we should keep an eye on,” Bailey wrote on July 22. “Outside of stars CeeDee Lamb and George Pickens, Dallas has zero depth at receiver. Ryan Flournoy, Marquez Valdes-Scantling, and Kavontae Turpin are the names behind the terrific duo — that simply won’t cut it. Signing Diggs as a chess piece who can move around both on the perimeter and the slot, just like Lamb and Pickens can do, would give the Cowboys the best trio of pass-catchers in the NFL.”

Signing Diggs at a cut-rate deal — think a 1-year, $10 million contract — gives incredible cover for Lamb, who has struggled with injuries, and Pickens, who only has 1 elite receiving season under his belt in 2025.

Diggs, of course, is the older brother of NFL All-Pro cornerback Trevon Diggs, who spent the 1st 6 seasons of his career in Dallas before he was released late in the 2025 regular season.

One popular school of thought has been that the Commanders will make a move to sign both of the Diggs siblings to bring them back close to home, where they grew up in Maryland.

The buzz only got louder in May, when a TikTok of the siblings together over the weekend showed them being asked: “How long until you both get in Commanders uniforms?”

Both brothers smiled widely at the question.

“That’s what everybody is talking about right now,” Stefon said. “We gonna talk about it real soon though.”

Stefon Diggs played for the New England Patriots in 202. He posted a 1,000-yard receiving season as he helped lead his team to a surprise appearance in the Super Bowl. In 11 seasons, Diggs is a 2-time NFL All-Pro and 4-time Pro Bowler. He has seven 1,000-yard receiving seasons in the last 8 years, interrupted only by a season-ending ACL injury while playing for the Houston Texans in 2024, and he was on pace to hit 1,000 receiving yards before his injury.

Pro Football Focus NFL reporter Bradley Locker called the Commanders the “Ideal Landing Spot” for Stefon Diggs on his list of the Top 10 remaining NFL free agents.

“The Commanders’ receiving corps was sneakily one of the more dire going into the draft,” Locker wrote. “Even after selecting Clemson’s Antonio Williams in the third round, Washington needs another reliable weapon for Jayden Daniels. Diggs was cut by the Patriots just one year into his lucrative contract, but his 2025 performance wasn’t the reason why.”

While off-field issues almost certainly led to Diggs’ early exit from New England, that’s not really an issue with the Cowboys. The most serious issue Diggs had hanging over his head was cleared up when he was found not guilty of assaulting his former private chef in May, and all of the other stuff kind of comes with the territory of not only being an NFL star but of playing for the Cowboys.

title: Hall of Fame Ex-Teammate Predicts LeBron James’ Next Team
description: A Hall of Fame former teammate has weighed in on where LeBron James should play next, offering a prediction about the NBA superstar's future. The post Hall of Fame Ex-Teammate Predicts LeBron James’ Next Team appeared first on HEAVY.
fullText:
LeBron James’ future remains one of the NBA’s biggest offseason storylines, and now one of his Hall of Famer former teammates has shared where he believes the four-time MVP should play next.

The recommendation reflects a broader vision for how James could shape the final chapter of his legendary career, but not everyone is likely to agree with it.

That teammate is Chris Bosh, James teammate for four years with the Miami Heat. Bosh’s advice to James? Return to Miami. Bosh has not spoken to James directly about the decision. That hasn’t stopped him from making the pitch publicly, delivered with the same dry humor that defined his playing days.

“When you’re older, when you’re ready to retire, you go to Miami,” Bosh joked in an interview with Front Office Sports. Bosh himself retired due to a health scare, making the 2015-16 season the last in which he played. The 6-foot-11 center was inducted into the Hall of Fame in 2021.

Bosh sees a Heat roster that could pair James with Bam Adebayo and the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade to Miami, comparing that frontcourt combination’s paint pressure to the dynamic he and Dwyane Wade once created running alongside James, leading to two NBA championships, in 2012 and 2013.

Read More From Heavy New NBA Exec Survey: LeBron James Will Snub Hometown Cavaliers For One Team

James, 41, told the Los Angeles Lakers in late June he would not return for 2026-27, informing the organization it could move forward without him this offseason, setting up a record 24th year in the league elsewhere.

The Cleveland Cavaliers, Golden State Warriors, and Philadelphia 76ers have all been floated as landing spots along with Miami. James has made no decision and set no deadline. Klutch Sports founder Rich Paul, James’ longtime agent, said on his own podcast this week that the process would not be rushed, framing it as a business decision rather than a spectacle, according to SI.com‘s reporting.

Bosh isn’t buying into the idea that legacy weighs on the decision at all.

“I think legacy and reputation, that’s already built,” Bosh said, according to Front Office Sports. “He’s earned the right to make his own decision.”

Bosh knows the free-agency dance because he lived it. Toronto drafted him fourth overall out of Georgia Tech in 2003, and he spent seven seasons building the Raptors into a playoff team before leaving for Miami in the 2010 free agency class that also landed James and Wade. The trio won back-to-back titles in 2012 and 2013.

Bosh made 11 All-Star teams and won Olympic gold with Team USA in 2008. His career ended prematurely in 2016 after recurring blood clots forced him off the floor for good. He officially retired in 2019.

Across 893 games, Bosh averaged 19.2 points and 8.5 rebounds while shooting 49.4 percent from the floor. He’s since written a book on athletic development and stays connected to the game through charity work, including a USA Basketball three-on-three event next month.

Whether James takes his old running mate’s advice remains to be seen. Miami, at least, has an alumnus lobbying for him.

title: Browns' Young Roster Lands Top 5 Recognition in New Ranking
description: Andrew Berry’s aggressive offseason saw the Cleveland Browns lean into their youth to transform the roster. His efforts to inject some young talent into the franchise didn't go unnoticed.
fullText:
Andrew Berry had a clear vision for the Cleveland Browns roster this offseason. After the franchise won just eight games in two seasons, Berry embraced a youth movement and turned the roster over by injecting young talent into it.

That started with Cleveland drafting four players in the first 60 picks of the NFL Draft, all four of whom are expected to contribute immediately. It was punctuated by the blockbuster trade that saw star edge rusher Myles Garrett shipped to the Los Angeles Rams for a haul of picks and young pass rusher Jared Verse.

Just like that, the Browns went from having one of the oldest teams in football to having one of the youngest, leaving plenty of room for excitement about the direction of the franchise in the coming years.

Berry's efforts did not go unnoticed, so much so that in a recent ranking by ESPN of the top teams based on talent under the age of 25, the Browns found themselves No. 4 overall, jumping over 25 spots from a season ago.

The explanation highlighted the offense first.

Let's start on offense, where the talent is really young. Fannin far outplayed what we usually see from a rookie tight end, catching 72 passes for 731 yards, and he just turned 22. Running back Quinshon Judkins is also 22, and his backup, Dylan Sampson , is 21. The Browns drafted two wide receivers this year: 21-year-old KC Concepcion and 22-year-old Denzel Boston . And this year's No. 9 pick, left tackle Fano, doesn't turn 22 until November.

Cleveland Browns Offseason Report; Which Brown is set for breakout? | Roster rankings fair or foul? The Cleveland Browns are just two weeks away from reporting to camp and then the season will be on for the Browns. Until then, it is another conversation abo...

Most of Berry's attention went to the offensive side of the football this offseason and rightfully so. The Browns were 30th in scoring in 2025 and struggled to sustain drives behind an old offensive line and with limited talent at the skill positions.

Meanwhile, the Browns defense was one of the best in football a season ago, but has plenty of promising young talent of its own. The piece highlighted that as well.

On defense, we start with Defensive Rookie of the Year Schwesinger, who turned 23 in February. Graham, last year's first-round pick, is 23. Second-round safety Emmanuel McNeil-Warren is 22 years old, and veteran safety Ronnie Hickman is still just 24 after three seasons in the NFL.

In the short term, boasting such a young team may not lead to many wins this season for Cleveland. But if its young core takes hold in the coming years, a new window of contention is on the horizon.

title: Mavericks match Knicks' offer sheet for Moussa Cisse, ink big man to two-year deal
description: New York forced Dallas' hand with an offer sheet and the Mavericks didn't wait long to make a decision.
fullText:
This story was originally published on July 18.

After a bit of uncertainty, Moussa Cisse is sticking around in Dallas for a while longer.

On Saturday morning, Cisse agreed to a two-year contract offer sheet with the NBA champion New York Knicks, meaning the Mavericks had 48 hours to match the Knicks' offer and keep the young center. Masai Ujiri and Dallas' front office didn't need the full 48 hours and matched New York's offer sheet on Saturday night, a person familiar with the situation confirmed to The Dallas Morning News. The team officially announced the move on Wednesday evening.

The Knicks' offer sheet to the restricted free agent was a two-year, $4.7 million deal with half the 2026-27 salary being guaranteed and the 2027-28 salary being non-guaranteed. The 6-foot-11 big man will now remain with the Mavericks on that same contract.

Cisse drew interest from New York thanks to his productive rookie season. While he only suited up in 38 games for the Mavs last season due to his two-way contract status, he averaged 4.5 points, 5.7 rebounds and 1.2 blocks in 13.9 minutes per game. Cisse flashed plenty of potential in that limited role, leading to outside interest that ultimately forced Dallas to match an offer sheet for the 23-year-old.

Instead of letting him get away to the Knicks, Mavericks will keep the talented young center as a piece in their rebuild around Cooper Flagg.

title: Phillies commit another ugly base running blunder against Dodgers
description: The Philadelphia Phillies continue to make questionable decisions while running the bases during their series with the Los Angeles Dodgers.
fullText:
The Philadelphia Phillies continue to make questionable decisions while running the bases during this week's series against the Los Angeles Dodgers at Citizens Bank Park in Philadelphia.

Trea Turner reached first base on a fielder's choice out in the bottom of the fifth ... only because Dodgers third baseman Max Muncy tagged out Bryson Stott, who for some reason decided to run from second to third with one out.

The game had just been tied 3-3 during the inning on a Bryan De La Cruz home run. But Stott's blunder halted the momentum with Kyle Schwarber popping out to end the inning.

“What are these guys doing.” 🤣

Man, the Phillies are ELITE at running the bases 🤯 pic.twitter.com/Pm7N6DjA4f

— SleeperDodgers (@SleeperDodgers) July 23, 2026

The Dodgers jumped back in front the next frame.

The Phillies' poor base running proved costly on Tuesday, July 21, as they lost 2-1 thanks to a disastrous final sequence.

Baserunning blunder to end the game. pic.twitter.com/56MXGdpfdO

— Rob Friedman (@PitchingNinja) July 22, 2026

title: Memphis Grizzlies moving on from former Ohio State star
description: Ohio State product D’Angelo Russell could be on the move again as Memphis works with him on a trade or buyout.
fullText:
A former Ohio State legend looks like he will be on the move.

According to a Yahoo Sports report, the Memphis Grizzlies and D'Angelo Russell are in discussions to work together to let the veteran guard find a new home.

The Memphis Grizzlies are undergoing a rebuild after the trade of their star Ja Morant this offseason and it does not seem to be a timeline fit with Russell. The sides are discussing a potential buyout agreement or possibly trade. A potential move would have Russell land with his fourth team in two seasons.

Russell was traded to the Grizzlies in a six-team trade earlier in the offseason. He spent time with the Dallas Mavericks and Washington Wizards last season.

The Memphis Grizzlies and veteran guard D’Angelo Russell are expected to work towards parting ways — either via buyout or trade — this offseason, sources tell @YahooSports. https://t.co/oLGlFcYA4R

— Kelly Iko (@KellyIko) July 23, 2026

Russell, 30, appeared in 25 games for Dallas in 2025-26. He chose to opt-in to the final year of his contract which was for $6 million dollars.

Russell played one season in Columbus before he declared for the 2015 NBA Draft. He was selected second overall by the Los Angeles Lakers. He played two seasons for Los Angeles before being moved to the Brooklyn Nets.

A former All-American, Russell has built himself a solid NBA career as a scorer and playmaker. His role has diminished later into his career and he will hope that whichever team he settles on next will offer him an opportunity to once again become a key role player and play for a championship.

title: Sophie Cunningham, Brittney Griner recreate pointing meme at Sun-Fever
description: The pointing meme strikes again!
fullText:
Indiana Fever star guard Sophie Cunningham once again brought back her notorious pointing meme with former Phoenix Mercury teammate and pal Brittney Griner on Wednesday night.

As Cunningham's Fever took on Griner's Connecticut Sun in Indianapolis, the two joked around pregame with Cunningham's famous pointing meme. We're pretty sure at this point Cunningham will be doing the pointing meme for the rest of her life; it's just broken out in a way most sports moments can't in this rampant attention economy.

title: Do Manchester United need to explore CB options?
description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup came to an end on Sunday with Spain reclaiming the title. They won a gritty matchup against Argentina with a 1-0 victory after extra time, and the physicality in the contest led to the early departure of Manchester United defender Lisandro Martinez. The “Butcher” picked up the injury playing his […]
fullText:
The 2026 FIFA World Cup came to an end on Sunday with Spain reclaiming the title. They won a gritty matchup against Argentina with a 1-0 victory after extra time, and the physicality in the contest led to the early departure of Manchester United defender Lisandro Martinez.

The “Butcher” picked up the injury playing his game, an aggressive and fearless approach to defending that sees him cover a lot of ground and make a lot of crunching tackles. His efforts have made him a fan favorite at Old Trafford as one of the few transfer hits from the Erik ten Hag era. His tenure at Old Trafford has not come without bumps and bruises, though, and his absences with bumps and bruises have kept him from playing his best for extended stretches.

With his injury, even expected to be minor, there is some concern over Manchester United’s depth at the position. Matthijs De Ligt in particular was one fans were worried about after a lengthy absence and operation to fix a back injury. He is expected back in October, but has already had some injury problems hinder his career to this point. He and Martinez both missed a lot of time last season due to injury.

The notable absences are papered over somewhat by the enduring presence of Harry Maguire, who somehow rediscovered the confidence and ability that made him one of the club’s most expensive signings in 2019. He too has had his share of knocks and absences, though, and is turning 34 next March.

The presence of Leny Yoro and Ayden Heaven also papered over them. In fact, the immediate answer to the center back depth questions is this: That’s what Leny Yoro and Ayden Heaven are there for. That isn’t an answer that inspires a ton of confidence given the club experienced the reality of multiple injuries shattering the defensive unit in the last two seasons. But if the club is betting on a return to full health for De Ligt and a swift return for Martinez, there isn’t a pressing need for a center back.

The answer may lie somewhere between Heaven and Johnny Evans, who is now on the coaching staff under Michael Carrick. A shrewd move for a promising young defender or a veteran option who is fine playing in League Cup and emergencies. That sort of player would be useful in a campaign where the Reds transition from playing 40 games total to at least 48 games.

Among the free agent options with Premier League experience, John Stones seems unlikely given his history at Manchester City and the wages it would cost. Axel Tuanzebe could be an option, but at 28 he is likely a player looking for regular first-team football.

The club has done well in the market by identifying youth players on short or expiring deals, players the original clubs would like to keep. If there is a center back option there or rising in the ranks at Carrington, that seems to be the best option currently.

title: ‘This Is Huge’ – NFL Podcaster Alleges New Evidence in Matt Miller Fantasy League Scandal
description: ESPN’s NFL Draft analyst Matt Miller has been making headlines recently, but it has had nothing to do with football. Instead, it’s because he’s being investigated by the Missouri attorney general for allegedly scamming his followers out of money he promised would go toward charity.
fullText:
ESPN’s NFL Draft analyst Matt Miller has been making headlines recently, but it has had nothing to do with football.

Instead, it’s because he’s being investigated by the Missouri attorney general for allegedly scamming his followers out of money he promised would go toward charity. This comes after a devastating car accident that required multiple surgeries and a life-saving amputation of his arm.

Now, one NFL podcaster provided some new information on Miller’s alleged scam, resurfacing audio of Miller’s brother potentially incriminating himself.

Earlier this month, the Missouri Attorney General’s Office opened an investigation into Miller. This was in response to numerous people coming forward and alleging that the ESPN analyst was scamming his followers using charity fantasy football leagues.

Miller allegedly started many leagues with an entry fee of $100, with half of the pot supposedly going to charity. However, many of the league’s winners never received their winnings, and there was no proof that the other half of the funds had been sent to charity. Miller was also accused of ghosting individuals who paid him for scouting lessons, but the current investigation focuses on the fantasy football leagues.

According to Awful Announcing, Miller was the commissioner of more than 91 leagues on Sleeper in 2025 alone before he was ultimately banned from the app.

After Miller was involved in a car accident with a semi-truck earlier this year, his family set up a GoFundMe for his followers to help with his medical bills. More than $50,000 was raised and prominent ESPN employees donated, including Pat McAfee, Adam Schefter, and Mina Kimes. However, the GoFundMe brought attention to a Reddit thread with the allegations against Miller, which then went viral on X as more accusations poured in.

REDRAFT THE ENTIRE NFL: PFN’s FREE NFL Ultimate Redraft Simulator

Now, NFL podcaster Tony Farmer believes he’s uncovered new evidence that links Miller’s brother, Marshal, to the scheme.

“Holy crap, I found it. Matt Miller’s brother Marshal acknowledging on tape he goes by ‘EmmandEmm.’ This is huge,” Farmer wrote on X. “Why it matters: 1) Now 2 different nicknames he goes by (also “melloesquire”) – often registered in the SAME Matt Miller charity fantasy leagues. Unless someone else was controlling these accounts with his alias, it appears Marshal Miller secretly controlled TWO teams in some of Miller’s leagues.

“Miller played in each league too so it appears the brothers sometimes controlled 3 of the 12 teams in the league! (There could be teams we don’t know too).”

Farmer further stated that Marshal Miller’s username “EmmandEmm” registered for 19 of Matt Miller’s fantasy leagues between 2024 and 2025. His other username, “MelloEsquire,” registered for 52 of Matt Miller’s fantasy leagues between 2024 and 2025, and both usernames were in the same league 12 times.

BE THE GM OF YOUR FAVORITE TEAM: PFN’s FREE NFL Mock Draft Simulator

“Remember that each owner was expected to pay $100 and half that money was promised to Matt for charity,” Farmer continued. “Do you think Marshal was paying registration fees? If the answer is no, then it raises questions about the actual amount of money that went to charity vs. what was promised.”

It’s unclear if this is accurate, but it’s just the latest aspect of this scandal that leads to more questions than answers. Miller is currently on a leave of absence at ESPN as he recovers from his accident.

Recently, Miller broke his silence on the allegations in an exchange with The Athletic.

“Because this is an active investigation, I’ve been told by legal counsel to limit my response,” Miller said in an email. “However, I’ve tried reaching out to all the winners from the last season to make sure they’ve been paid.

“My [direct messages] on all social media remain open for winners to contact me. Because of my limited access to my original email account, I have had trouble authenticating winners, but everyone who finished in first, second, or third place will be paid out, and I’ve worked hard to make that happen.”

Real Salt Lake can leapfrog Los Angeles FC in the Western Conference with a win at BMO Stadium on Wednesday. However, the Claret and Cobalt will need their first road win away to LAFC in almost three years to do so.

This will be the first fixture Pablo Mastroeni’s men have played since Major League Soccer broke for the World Cup two months ago. title: Azzi Fudd flaunts sporty-streetwear outfit for Dallas Wings game
description: Azzi Fudd’s pregame outfits have been a must-see this season. She once again turned heads with her attire on Wednesday ahead of the Dallas Wings-Portland Fire WNBA regular-season game. The rookie wore a fitted, white cropped sports bra top featuring…
fullText:
Azzi Fudd’s pregame outfits have been a must-see this season. She once again turned heads with her attire on Wednesday ahead of the Dallas Wings-Portland Fire WNBA regular-season game.

The rookie wore a fitted, white cropped sports bra top featuring a subtle black logo in the center of the chest. She paired it with high-waisted khaki track pants with white vertical side stripes down the outer legs and a drawstring waist. Fudd completed her look with white sneakers.

MORE: Azzi Fudd bares abs ahead of Wings-Tempo game

The Wings posted her photo from the tunnel on Instagram, and fans reacted to it in the comments section.

“I need azzis abs by tomorrow,” one fan said.

“Wait a minute, Azzi body is teaaa 🔥,” one user wrote.

“Azzi is dressed in light clothing,” posted another.

“Azzi’s abs are so insane they look like they’re cussing me out through the screen. 😩,” another fan commented.

“Awwwww so cuteee,” one comment read.

The No. 1 pick of the 2026 draft is averaging 13.4 points, 1.6 rebounds, 2.4 assists and 1.6 steals in her last five games. However, what stands out is her 3-point shooting, knocking down 12 of 30 shots for 40.0%.

Fudd is strengthening her case for the Rookie of the Year award. Olivia Miles is widely considered the leader in the race for the honor, but the Dallas guard is closing the gap. Fudd and Co. have won six of their last seven games and are 17-9 for the season.

title: 'Love the way the guy plays': Nick Saban gives high praise to Texas A&M QB Marcel Reed
description: Legendary head coach and current ESPN analyst Nick Saban says he loves Texas A&M quarterback Marcel Reed and what Mike Elko has done in College Station.
fullText:
One of college football's most legendary head coaches gave much praise to Texas A&M head coach Mike Elko and his quarterback Marcel Reed in an interview with ESPN from the 2026 SEC Media Days.

Former Alabama head coach and current college football analyst Nick Saban said he really likes what the Aggies bring to the table this season, including the return of Reed as the signal-caller. Texas A&M achieved its first 11-0 start since 1992 and reached the College Football Playoff for the first time in Elko's second season at the helm, paving the way for a higher ceiling and much higher expectations.

According to Saban, Reed's a really good passer who has continued to get better each year. With a slew of talented and electric targets surrounding him, this could be the Nashville, Tennessee, native's chance to take his game to the next level.

Should expectations be high for Marcel Reed and Texas A&M? 🤔 pic.twitter.com/sD4FNOB9ss

— ESPN College Football (@ESPNCFB) July 22, 2026

It is undeniable that Saban is a believer in what Elko has built in College Station, after being ranked second in program history for best record through the first two seasons (19-7), just behind former Aggie head coach Kevin Sumlin. Texas A&M also tied a school record with 10 players selected in the 2026 NFL Draft.

Despite the loss of production, the Aggies hauled in the No. 4 transfer portal class, No. 7 recruiting class and retained guys like Reed, safety Marcus Ratcliffe and linebacker Daymion Sanford, all of which represented the Maroon and White at SEC Media Days on Wednesday.

Between the talent on the roster, coaching staff, stadium atmosphere and passionate fan base, Texas A&M has all the ingredients to become one of college football's most elite programs, but they must be able to raise the product on the field to the next level, facing one of the most daunting schedules in the nation.

Contact/Follow us @AggiesWire on X (formerly Twitter) and like our page on Facebook to follow ongoing coverage of Texas A&M news, notes and opinions. Follow Dylan on X: @dylanmflippo.

This article originally appeared on Aggies Wire: 'Love the way the guy plays': Nick Saban gives high praise to Texas A&M QB Marcel Reed

title: 76ers Get Encouraging LeBron James Signal From Rival’s Admission
description: The 76ers received a potential boost in the LeBron James sweepstakes as a rival expressed growing doubt about its chances. The post 76ers Get Encouraging LeBron James Signal From Rival’s Admission appeared first on HEAVY.
fullText:
The Philadelphia 76ers remain behind the Golden State Warriors in the betting market for LeBron James, but the latest message from inside Golden State could provide Philadelphia with a potential opening.

ESPN Warriors insider Anthony Slater said Wednesday on NBA Today that Golden State is pessimistic about its chances of signing James. The Warriors have kept a roster spot open and would welcome the four-time NBA champion, but they do not believe his decision is leaning in their direction.

“They’ve just not thought they’re at the top of the list,” Slater said. “I talked to somebody last night that basically said they’ve given no sign or hint that it’s going to happen.”

Slater noted that Stephen Curry has contacted James directly and Draymond Green recently vacationed with him. Those relationships should give Golden State some insight into James’ thinking.

“I would think that the Warriors would know if it’s leaning one way or the other,” Slater said. “And all of my indications from the Warriors’ side is they believe it is not leaning toward them.”

That does not mean James is leaning toward Philadelphia. But one prominent rival’s declining confidence represents potentially positive news for the 76ers.

The latest consensus odds, according to The Athletic, listed Golden State at +550 to sign James, with Philadelphia behind the Warriors at +950.

Miami was the favorite at minus-125, followed by Cleveland at +240. Those numbers shifted after the Heat mistakenly published and deleted a YouTube link labeled “LeBron James Introductory Press Conference.”

Miami said the link was created as part of its contingency preparations and that its social media staff had no advance knowledge of James’ decision.

Betting odds measure market activity and implied probability. They do not necessarily reflect James’ private preferences or the information available to each organization.

Golden State’s internal pessimism therefore does not automatically move Philadelphia ahead of the Warriors. It does, however, suggest that one of the teams priced above the Sixers has not received a favorable signal despite direct recruiting efforts from two of James’ closest NBA peers.

Philadelphia’s case rests on a reconfigured core featuring Joel Embiid, Jaylen Brown and Tyrese Maxey. VJ Edgecombe gives the roster another athletic young guard, while James would provide the connective playmaking needed to organize the Sixers’ scorers.

LeBron James defends 76ers guard Tyrese Maxey during a regular-season matchup between the Lakers and Philadelphia. The Sixers have slipped behind the Warriors in the latest LeBron James next team odds as his free agency watch continues into its third week.

Philadelphia also possesses an important personal connection through Maxey, who shares an agent with James.

Rich Paul, the founder of Klutch Sports, reecently identified Maxey as the starting point for any Sixers pitch to James on his Game Over podcast with Max Kellerman.

“If there is a draw from the Philadelphia 76ers, of course it starts with Tyrese Maxey, absolutely,” Paul said. “Why? Not just because I represent both players. Representation aside, when you look at a guy like Tyrese Maxey, what do you see?”

Paul praised Maxey’s personality, durability and influence within the organization.

“First of all, I’ve never seen a guy play 48 minutes smiling,” Paul said. “If you talk to people around the organization, since he’s now the face of that organization, it’s changed. That’s a positive, for sure.”

Paul cautioned that Maxey would not be the only factor James considers.

“But, again, if that is someone that you’re inquiring about, of course it has to start there,” Paul said. “But that’s not the only thing.”

Sixers president Bob Myers has separately argued that Philadelphia offers James his best opportunity to win a fifth championship. Myers made his case on another episode of the same podcast, providing James’ agent with a direct explanation of the Sixers’ appeal.

Salary should not distinguish the contenders. Paul told Kendrick Perkins that money is not driving the process because the teams pursuing James can offer the same contract—the veteran minimum.

Philadelphia still has no assurance that it is gaining ground. Slater’s reporting only explains how Golden State views its chances.

But free agent pursuits are relative. If one competitor ahead of Philadelphia believes James is leaning elsewhere—and James’ agent sees Maxey as a genuine draw—the Sixers have at least two reasons to remain hopeful.

Like HEAVY's content? Be sure to follow us.

This article was originally published on HEAVY

title: 2027 NFL Draft Summer Scouting: Kenyatta Jackson Jr., Edge, Ohio State
description: The Ohio State defense is among the best in the country, but what impact does Kenyatta Jackson Jr. have?
fullText:
Summer is here, and it is everyone’s favorite time of the year, scouting time. Every year, we do Summer Scouting to preview college football for Vikings fans, but also give them an idea of names to follow and watch in the fall as needs pop up for Minnesota.

Are we going to write about quarterbacks? Offensive tackles? Edge rushers? You know it, but why? Because other NFL teams will draft them, just as they will draft every other position. It all matters whether these players end up on the Vikings, the Bears, or the Steelers. We write these so Vikings fans can be the most informed fans.

So if you are still with us, enjoy our latest Summer Scouting report as we prepare you for the Fall.

4-Star prospect according to 247 Sports

Named the Gatorade Player of the Year in the state of Florida

Under Armour All-American in high school

Lines up the most at REO (37% of snaps)

2 Passes batted at the line of scrimmage

One of the better edges in run support

Great tackling technique, wraps up

Ideal size and length to make an impact

Can win with his hand in the dirt

Tremendous power to knock blockers back

Kenyatta Jackson Jr. is a more throwback type of player at the edge rusher position. He strikes me as someone who can win off the edge in a 3-4 scheme and also provide excellent run support. He may not ever be a big sack producer, but he can start, be in packages at the goal line, and provide pressure time and again. He is a safe evaluation already, in my opinion, but he does have some potential to take another step in 2026.

This article originally appeared on Vikings Wire: 2027 NFL Draft Summer Scouting: Kenyatta Jackson Jr., Edge, Ohio State

title: Aliff Sor Dechapan Receives Backing From Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim Ahead Of World Title Fight On July 24
description: Before The Inner Circle 23 on Friday, hear what the Malaysian Prime Minister had to say about the young star!
fullText:
Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim has rallied the nation behind Aliff Sor Dechapan, who stands one victory away from becoming his nation’s first-ever World Champion in ONE Championship.

Aliff will square off against the legendary Sam-A Gaiyanghadao for the ONE Interim Strawweight Muay Thai World Title in the main event of The Inner Circle 23, which goes down live on Friday, July 24.

The card will stream live in Asia primetime from Bangkok’s iconic Lumpinee Stadium, and it will be exclusively available to subscribers at live.onefc.com.

This is not the first time the Prime Minister has thrown his support behind one of Malaysia’s athletes in the world’s largest martial arts organization.

Anwar welcomed teenage knockout machine Johan “Jojo” Ghazali to the Prime Minister’s Office in Putrajaya last October, a meeting that underscored his backing for the country’s combat sports scene.

Now, that same support is being extended to Aliff, who stands on the precipice of becoming the first Malaysian ever to hold the coveted ONE World Title belt.

This is no small moment for a country whose presence in the global martial arts community continues to grow at a rapid pace.

Malaysia has produced a wave of promising talent in recent years, but a World Championship has remained just out of reach, making the 22-year-old’s opportunity inside Lumpinee Stadium a potential turning point for the bustling nation.

That significance has clearly not gone unnoticed at the highest levels of government. With the entire country watching, Anwar has chosen to lend his voice publicly in support of Aliff’s bid for history.

The Prime Minister took to his official Instagram account to voice his support for the young gun, urging fellow Malaysians to tune in for what could be a landmark sporting moment in the country.

“Let us rally behind Ahmad Nor Iman Aliff Rakib as he competes for the ONE Interim Strawweight Muay Thai World Championship title this Friday.

“Aliff Rakib has made history as the first Malaysian combat athlete to qualify for a shot at this title. May he continue to bring glory to Malaysia and inspire the younger generation to achieve success on a global stage.”

Riding that wave of national support, Aliff Sor Dechapan has made it clear his pursuit of gold extends well beyond personal glory.

For an athlete who has carried his country’s flag since debuting on the global stage in April 2023, this opportunity represents something far bigger than his own legacy.

Just days out from the biggest fight of his career, the Sor Dechapan representative made his motivation known to the entire nation watching.

The 22-year-old took to his Instagram account and wrote:

“I am not fighting for myself alone. I am fighting for my country. I have one dream – to bring the belt home and make history.

“I hope for the prayers and support of the entire nation. God willing, I will give my all to become the first fighter to bring that championship home for my country.”

Aliff heads into this monumental ONE Interim Strawweight Muay Thai World Title clash at The Inner Circle 23 on a five-bout winning streak.

Most recently, the Kuala Lumpur-based star earned the 62nd win of his career with a unanimous decision victory over Ramadan “The Scorpion” Ondash at ONE Fight Night 38 last December.

It also marked the ninth triumph of his promotional campaign, one filled with several knockouts against other world-class fighters.

Sam-A Gaiyanghadao represents his toughest test yet, but few warriors have looked this composed on the doorstep of history.

title: Yankees offense scores late, salvage Max Fried's fantastic return in Game 2 win over Pirates
description: Max Fried made a triumphant return, pitching five scoreless and the Yankees lineup woke up late to defeat the Pirates, 2-0, in the second game of Wednesday's doubleheader.
fullText:
Max Fried made a triumphant return, pitching five scoreless and the Yankees lineup woke up late to defeat the Pirates, 2-0, in the second game of Wednesday's doubleheader.

-Fried faced the minimum in the first thanks to Cody Bellinger gunning down Nick Gonzales trying to stretch a single into a double to lead off the game. The southpaw was locked in from there, allowing just a walk across the next two innings. Fried got through five innings against a formidable Pirates lineup, allowing just one hit and one walk.

The plan was for Fried to throw 60 pitches, and he wound up tossing 57 (37 strikes) while striking out seven batters.

-Unfortunately, the Yankees lineup was anemic once again for most of the game.

Against young right-hander Bubba Chandler, the Yankees mustered just two hits and got just two base runners in scoring position through the first four innings.

Chandler entered Game 2 with a 4.77 ERA. He also entered the game with a 13 percent walk rate, but the Yankees could only work one walk against him. The youngster pitched 6.1 scoreless innings on 84 pitches, allowing two hits, one walk and striking out five batters. His ERA lowered to 4.47.

-The Yankees lineup would finally break through against the Pirates bullpen in the seventh. After Cody Bellinger walked, Jasson Dominguez -- hitting from the right side -- lined a double to score the game's first run. Anthony Volpe followed up with a single, scoring Dominguez from second. Both hits came with two outs.

-The Pirates had their best chance in the seventh after Volpe's miscue allowed the leadoff hitter to reach against Angel Chivilli. Paul Blackburn came in after and worked around an error from catcher Ali Sanchez -- not helped by Volpe not blocking -- and a walk to keep the game scoreless by striking out Ryan O'Hearn and Marcell Ozuna.

Blackburn would pitch a scoreless eighth before Will Warren, Friday's probable starter, came out for the ninth. After allowing a leadoff hit, Warren retired the next two batters but walked Esmerlyn Valdez. Aaron Boone called on Tim Hill to face the lefty O'Hearn. O'Hearn grounded to Jazz Chisholm Jr., who double-clutched before slinging it to first. It was called an out on the field and after a review, the call on the field stood, ending the game.

The veteran right-hander not only kept the game scoreless, but also delivered two innings for a bullpen that needed the length.

Max Fried is dominating in his return to the mound 🔥 pic.twitter.com/pgJ5zOA3HM

— SNY Yankees (@snyyankees) July 23, 2026

Ryan McMahon with the spin-o-rama! pic.twitter.com/l1KPFyZ7Km

— SNY Yankees (@snyyankees) July 23, 2026

Paul Blackburn gets out of trouble in the 7th! pic.twitter.com/sCKWROMdYB

— SNY Yankees (@snyyankees) July 23, 2026

THE MARTIAN DELIVERS.

Jasson Domínguez brings home Cody Bellinger! 💪 pic.twitter.com/hLd7AJH6fO

— SNY Yankees (@snyyankees) July 23, 2026

Anthony Volpe doubles the lead! pic.twitter.com/6ouJxCD9EY

— SNY Yankees (@snyyankees) July 23, 2026

After an off day, the Yankees head to Philadelphia to start a three-game series with the Phillies on Friday. First pitch is set for 6:45 p.m.

Will Warren (7-4, 4.03 ERA) will take the mound and go up against Jesus Luzardo (9-4, 3.43 ERA).
